name: Isábena Valley - Congosto and Monastery of Obarra and Roda de Isábena
name_original: Valle de Isábena - Congosto y Monasterio de Obarra y Roda de Isábena
region_intro: Road cycling around Capella features a landscape characterized by rolling hills and river valleys, with several routes traversing rural roads and passing by significant water bodies. The region includes areas like the Sierra de Laguarres and the Barasona Reservoir, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Elevation gains are common, providing a mix of challenging climbs and more gradual sections. Best road cycling routes around Capella The most popular road cycling route is Tour of Isábena from Graus, a 52.8 miles (84.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through the Isábena valley. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Laguarres Loop from Graus, a difficult 29.2 miles (47.1 km) path. This loop offers climbs through the Sierra de Laguarres, providing panoramic views. Local road cyclists also love the Tour around the Barasona Reservoir from Graus, a 13.9 miles (22.4 km) trail leading through reservoir landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 2 minutes. Road cycling around Capella is defined by rural roads, reservoir circuits, and climbs through local sierras. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Capella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from 8 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Capella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Capella?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Capella, offering a diverse range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes over 800 times, highlighting the region's popularity among cyclists.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Capella?
A: Yes, Capella offers 4 easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924205531/tour-around-the-barasona-reservoir-from-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the Barasona Reservoir from Graus&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.9 miles (22.4 km) long and features minimal elevation gain, allowing you to enjoy the scenic reservoir landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Capella?
A: The terrain around Capella is characterized by rolling hills and river valleys, with many routes traversing rural roads. You'll encounter a mix of challenging climbs, particularly in areas like the Sierra de Laguarres, and more gradual sections along reservoir circuits. This variety provides options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there challenging routes for advanced road cyclists in Capella?
A: Absolutely. Capella is home to 26 difficult road cycling routes. For a significant challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924127206/cajigar-loop-from-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cajigar Loop from Graus&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 82.1 km and includes over 1000 meters of elevation gain, offering a demanding ride through the region's varied landscape.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or attractions I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer picturesque views, especially those traversing the Sierra de Laguarres or circling the Barasona Reservoir. You can also explore historical and natural landmarks. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5088743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capella Romanesque Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the charming village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3521437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roda de Isábena&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4443331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilica of the Virgen de la Peña&lt;/a&gt;. For those interested in history, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4762285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Perrarúa&lt;/a&gt; is also accessible.

Q: Are there any loop trails for road cycling in Capella?
A: Yes, many of Capella's road cycling routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924144375/sierra-de-laguarres-loop-from-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Laguarres Loop from Graus&lt;/a&gt;, which offers climbs and panoramic views over 47.1 km. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924170502/el-grado-dam-and-barasona-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Grado Dam and Barasona Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 53.4 km.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Capella?
A: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Capella, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summer can be warm, especially in the valleys, while winter might bring cooler temperatures, though many routes remain accessible.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Capella?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from 8 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, from challenging climbs in the sierras to scenic rides around reservoirs, and the quiet rural roads that define the region's cycling experience.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by significant water bodies?
A: Yes, several routes in Capella feature significant water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924205531/tour-around-the-barasona-reservoir-from-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the Barasona Reservoir from Graus&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering beautiful views of the reservoir. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924170502/el-grado-dam-and-barasona-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Grado Dam and Barasona Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, also incorporates these scenic water features.

Q: What is the typical duration of road cycling routes in Capella?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and elevation gain. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924205531/tour-around-the-barasona-reservoir-from-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the Barasona Reservoir from Graus&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour. More challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927649875/tour-of-isabena-from-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Isábena from Graus&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 4 hours due to their length (84.9 km) and significant elevation.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles to explore near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region around Capella is rich in history. You can find several historical sites and castles. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4762285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Perrarúa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2905086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Village of Castarlenas&lt;/a&gt; offer glimpses into the past. Further afield, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5271963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Benabarre&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable historical landmark.
tips: Narrow gorge drawn by the Isábena river, it can be a good alternative to access the Benasque valley.
This spectacular monastery, a Lombard work from the beginning of the 11th century, has been declared a historical-artistic monument.

Here is the ex-cathedral of San Vicente, considered the oldest in Aragon and the smallest in Spain, as well as one of the most beautiful. The cathedral began to be built in the 11th century in the Lombard Romanesque style and over the centuries it has undergone several reconstructions and extensions. Its beautiful cloister, austere and small, is a charming place for a walk.

waypoints.name: Cathedral of San Vicente and Cloister of Roda de Isábena
